IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: To provide development opportunities that empower young people to create positive change.
IRS990.MissionDesc: Junior Chamber International (JCI), Inc., (The Organization") is a federation of independent national service organizations of members from different countries around the world. The Organization's primary purpose is to contribute to the advancement of the global community by providing the opportunity for young people to develop the leadership skills, social responsibility, fellowship, and entrepreneurship necessary to create positive change.
IRS990.Desc: JCI Events: Provides for travel and meeting expenses in connection with the Organization's exempt purpose, including Area Conferences and World Congress.

ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp.Desc: Membership Development and Support: which includes expenses associated with providing administrative support to JCI National Organizations in the implementation of their projects that fulfill their mission, the creation of new organizations, and the expansion of existing organizations, implementation of growth strategy initiatives, the administration of auxiliary membership groups such as Senate and Alumni, and the creation of membership guides and tools

ProgSrvcAccomActy3Grp.Desc: Special Events: which includes expenses associated with National Presidents Meetings, Partner Events, Kanazawa Conference, and other miscellaneous events organized by or involving JCI.

ProgSrvcAccomActyOtherGrp.Desc: Other Initiatives: which includes expenses associated with Skills Development course creation, development, and speaker management, Global Youth Empowerment Fund, Campaign & Advocacy (ex. Peace is Possible campaign), and Special Impact Projects (ex. Development Grants)

OtherSecuritiesGrp.Desc: Beneficial Interest in Jaycees International Foundation
Cash Held on Behalf of Third Party

OtherLiabilitiesOrgGrp.Desc: Agency Funds Due to Third Parties

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The intended use of the Organization's Endowment Funds is to support the programs and activities of JCI.

The footnote regarding FIN 48 (ASC 740) provided by the taxpayer's audited financial statements states as follows: "The Organization is a nonprofit organization and is exempt from income tax under Section 504(c)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code. Therefore, no provision for federal, state, or local income taxes has been included in these financial statements. ASC 740, Income Taxes, clarifies the accounting for uncertainty in income taxes recognized in the Organization's financial statements and prescribes a recognition threshold and measurement attribute for the financial statement recognition and measurement of a tax position taken or expected to be taken in a tax return. ASC 740 also provides guidance on derecognition and measurement of a tax position taken or expected to be taken in a tax return. The Organization files annual information returns with the federal government. The Organization's federal information returns for years 2015 and beyond remain subject to examination by the Internal Revenue Service. The Organization did not have unrecognized tax benefits as of December 31, 2018 and does not expect this to change significantly over the next 12 months. The Organization will recognize interest and penalties accrued on any unrecognized tax benefits as a component of income tax expense. As of December 31, 2018, the Organization has not accrued interest or penalties related to uncertain tax positions."
Book Earnings of Jaycees International Foundation 188,455. Cost of Sales Reported on Revenue Statement 52,932. Loss on Disposal of Assets 20,905.

Cost of Sales Reported on Revenue Statement 52,932. Foreign Currency Loss 9,609. Loss on Disposal of Assets 20,905. Unrealized Loss on Investments 122,161. Realized Gain on Investments (Reported Net with Unrealized on FS) -12,881.

AccountActivitiesOutsideUSGrp.TypeOfActivitiesConductedTxt: Employees perform membership services and administrative support duties.
Employees perform membership services and administrative support duties.
Employees perform membership services and administrative support duties.
Employees perform membership services and administrative support duties.

GrantsToOrgOutsideUSGrp.PurposeOfGrantTxt: Civic Service Projects in Respective Areas.
Civic Service Projects in Respective Areas.
Civic Service Projects in Respective Areas.
Civic Service Projects in Respective Areas.

ForeignIndividualsGrantsGrp.TypeOfAssistanceTxt: Reimbursements to World President and Board Members for travel related expenses

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Grants are issued to regional JCI Chambers for specific grants submitted to the World Wide headquarters.
Number of participants represents the members of the Board of Directors that are citizens of foreign countries.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section A, line 6
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Organization has members who pay membership fees.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section A, line 7a
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Members annually elect Board of Directors and Officers.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, line 11b
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Copies of the final Form 990 and schedules are emailed to each U.S. board member prior to being filed with the IRS. Board members are asked to review the return individually and to contact the return preparer, other board members, or executive officers with any questions or concerns.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, line 12c
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: If anyone voices a concern, it is investigated and resolved.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, line 15
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: There is an annual Salary Ranges Review Committee meeting held at World Congress which covers salaries for all employees except the Secretary General. The Salary Ranges Review Committee is made up of the President, Immediate Past President, Treasurer and Secretary General. The Secretary General's contract is initially established per JCI Constitution and Policy 16-1 and his performance is reviewed by the Executive Committee of the Board of Directors annually at World Congress.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section C, line 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: All organization governing documents, conflict of interest policy, and financial statements are made available to the public upon written request.
Book Earnings of Jaycees International Foundation 188,455. Foreign Currency Loss -9,609.
Organization's Secretary General reviews the audit of the financial statements for accuracy and completeness on behalf of the organizations Executive Committee.

IdRelatedTaxExemptOrgGrp.PrimaryActivitiesTxt: The purpose of furthering charitable aims and objectives of JCI.
